AuthorizationRequest.description:
The IdP authorize URL for one freshly-opened flow — the client navigates to it.

The binding secrets (``state`` server-side lookup key aside, the ``nonce`` and the
PKCE verifier) stay server-side in the state store; only the URL leaves.
AuthorizationRequest.title: AuthorizationRequest

CurrentUser.description:
The authenticated caller's own identity — the ``/me`` (who-am-I) response.

The frontend's session contract pairs this with :class:`AccessToken`. It is the
server-validated current identity (resolved through the wired principal provider —
the revocable one in the bundled stack), so it reflects the live store, not just the
token's claims. The caller's role is on the wire as both the numeric ``role_rank``
(the comparable primitive, ADR 0004 / 0022) and a human-readable ``role_name``.
CurrentUser.title: CurrentUser

FileRead.description: The file metadata as returned by the API — **without** ``storage_key`` / ``storage_profile``.
FileRead.title: FileRead

FileUpdate.description:
Patch a file's mutable metadata (OCC via the inherited required ``version``).

Only ``filename`` is patchable; the content-derived fields (``content_type`` /
``size`` / ``sha256`` / ``storage_key``) are append-only — replacing content is a new
upload, never an edit.

version.description: Version from the most recent read response, used for optimistic concurrency control. A stale value is rejected with HTTP 409.
version.title: Version

FileUpdate.title: FileUpdate

OIDCCallbackRequest.description: What the IdP appended to the redirect URI, relayed by the client.
OIDCCallbackRequest.title: OIDCCallbackRequest

version.description: Version from the most recent read response, used for optimistic concurrency control. A stale value is rejected with HTTP 409.
version.title: Version

version.description: Version from the most recent read response, used for optimistic concurrency control. A stale value is rejected with HTTP 409.
version.title: Version

UserAdminUpdate.description:
Admin edits a user's email / role rank (OCC ``version`` required).

Activation state is changed through the dedicated ``deactivate`` /
``reactivate`` actions, not this generic patch.

version.description: Version from the most recent read response, used for optimistic concurrency control. A stale value is rejected with HTTP 409.
version.title: Version

UserAdminUpdate.title: UserAdminUpdate

UserPasswordReset.description: Admin sets a new password for a user.
UserPasswordReset.title: UserPasswordReset

UserProvision.description:
Admin-provisions a new user with an initial password and a role *rank*.

``role`` is an integer rank resolved against the app's ``PermissionModel`` at
login (ADR 0022), so an admin can provision any role the app's ladder defines —
not only the default three tiers; an unmodeled rank simply fails closed at that
user's next login. The default is the viewer rank.
UserProvision.title: UserProvision

WebhookDeliveryRead.description: One immutable delivery attempt, as returned by the read-only delivery log.
WebhookDeliveryRead.title: WebhookDeliveryRead

WebhookSubscriptionCreate.description: Register a webhook. ``secret`` is supplied here (input only) and never returned.
WebhookSubscriptionCreate.title: WebhookSubscriptionCreate

WebhookSubscriptionRead.description: The subscription as returned by the API — deliberately **without** ``secret``.
WebhookSubscriptionRead.title: WebhookSubscriptionRead

WebhookSubscriptionUpdate.description: Patch a webhook (optimistic concurrency via the inherited required ``version``).

version.description: Version from the most recent read response, used for optimistic concurrency control. A stale value is rejected with HTTP 409.
version.title: Version

WebhookSubscriptionUpdate.title: WebhookSubscriptionUpdate

parameters.description: Filter to users whose email contains this text (case-insensitive).
parameters.name: email

get.description: Liveness: the process is up. No dependency is checked.
Readiness: 200 when the database answers ``SELECT 1``, else 503.
